---
title: "Oxygen-Isotopic Expression of Forced Changes in the Pacific Walker Circulation"
subtitle: "Chapter 1 — dissertation talk"
author:
  - Cali Pfleger
  - Samantha Stevenson
  - Sloan Coats
  - Bronwen Knockey
  - Georgina Falster
date: last-modified
bibliography: ../literature/bibliography.bib
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    footer: "Ch 1 · iLME PWC · calipfleger@ucsb.edu"
    incremental: false
    smaller: false
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Motivation

The **Pacific Walker Circulation (PWC)** is a critical regulator of global precipitation.

- Tightly coupled to ENSO.
- Quantifying GHG vs. aerosol sensitivity matters for climate projection.
- **Observational records are too short** to constrain its anthropogenic response directly.

. . .

**Idea:** use the isotope-enabled CESM Last Millennium Ensemble to read the forced response in $\delta^{18}\text{O}_p$ — a variable we can compare directly with paleoclimate proxies.

## The debate

Climate models disagree on whether the PWC has strengthened or weakened.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Model projections** (weakening):

- Reduced convective mass flux under GHG warming [@vecchi2006weakening; @heldsoden2006robust].
- Expected El Niño–like SST response.
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Recent observations** (strengthening):

- Stronger trade winds since ~1980 [@lheureux2013recent; @sohn2013strengthening].
- La Niña–like SST pattern [@tokinaga2012slowdown].
:::
:::

. . .

Models *systematically fail* to reproduce the observed strengthening [@wills2021systematic].

## Research questions

1. Can we resolve the forced PWC response by decomposing the iLME into **GHG-only** vs. **ozone-aerosol-only** single-forcing ensembles?
2. Does **$\delta^{18}\text{O}_p$** provide information that SLP + SST alone do not?
3. Which mechanism — thermodynamic warming or aerosol-driven dynamic cooling — dominates the observed isotopic signature?

## Dataset: CESM-LME with isotopes

Isotope-enabled Community Earth System Model Last Millennium Ensemble (iCESM / CESM-LME) [@brady2019icesm].

| Forcing | Members | iCESM subset |
|---|---|---|
| Full forcing | 17 | 4 |
| GHG only | 7 | 4 |
| Ozone + aerosol | 8 | 5 |

Focus: **post-industrial era 1850–2005 CE**. Variables: TS, SLP, U/V wind, $\delta^{18}\text{O}_p$.

## PWC index — classical definition

$$\text{PWC}_{\text{SLP}} = \overline{\text{SLP}}_{\text{CEP}} - \overline{\text{SLP}}_{\text{WP}}$$

- **CEP** = central–east Pacific (150°–90° W, 5° S–5° N).
- **WP** = western Pacific (90°–150° E, 5° S–5° N).

Compared against the **Niño 3.4** index [@vecchi2006weakening; @tokinaga2012slowdown; @falster2021precipitation].

## A new $\delta^{18}\text{O}_p$-based index

Incorporating $\delta^{18}\text{O}_p$ gives a moisture-transport perspective SLP/SST can't see, and bridges modeling with paleoclimate proxies [@dee2018vertical; @konecky2020iso2k].

## Figures {.scrollable}

Figures from `chapter-1-ilme-pwc/figures/` appear here. Phase 5 of the nightly pipeline auto-adds new figures with captions. Chapter 1 has pre-written captions in `drafts/from-drive/ch1-paper-figure-captions.md`.

<!--
When figures exist, they show up like this:

## SLP linear trend under full forcing

![SLP linear trend 1850–2005 — full-forcing ensemble.](../figures/fig_slp_trend_full.png){.r-stretch}

The nightly pipeline inserts these automatically.
-->

_Placeholder — no figures committed to the repo yet. Run your analysis scripts in `code/` to produce outputs under `figures/`, then the nightly pipeline populates slides here._

## Key findings (preview)

- **OZA forcing → strengthened PWC** (La Niña–like): seen in vertical velocity, SLP, precipitation, $\delta^{18}\text{O}_p$.
- **GHG forcing → weakened PWC** (El Niño–like): warmer SSTs and enriched $\delta^{18}\text{O}_p$ globally.
- **Combined** ≈ Full forcing.

. . .

**The isotopic fingerprint discriminates forcings in a way SLP and SST alone cannot.**

## Open questions / Next steps

- $\delta\text{D}$ cross-section tests of tropospheric static stability [@heldsoden2006robust].
- Longitudinal $\delta\text{D}_{\text{vapor}}$ at 500–600 hPa [@dee2018vertical].
- Regression on PC1 of $\delta^{18}\text{O}_p$ compared to Iso2k effective-moisture PC1 [@konecky2020iso2k].

## References {.scrollable}

::: {#refs}
:::
